speaker: Housing Judge
description: Voice-reference dialogue samples for the Housing Judge in Pig & Swine RPG. These are draft samples, not final committed game text.
source_role_summary: Tests rule clarity, factual basis, targeted enforcement, and proportionality
Balances landlord management rights with home and expression concerns
Rewards specific evidence: poster photo, warning letters, building rules, witnesses, complaint log

voice_profile.core_voice: measured, humane, rigorous, fact-sensitive, procedurally disciplined, quietly dry
comedy_engine: stays serious while absurd poster and façade language collapses under scrutiny
dryly refuses to let parties turn aesthetic dislike into legal principle
rewards evidence that makes home impact concrete
becomes mildly exasperated by vague rules, vague complaints, and theatrical overreach
lets human stakes matter without letting sentiment replace proof
do: make the judge fair to both landlord and tenant
distinguish dislike, disturbance, breach, and eviction
keep remedies realistic: suspension, reconsideration, clearer reasons, sometimes stronger protection
do_not: make the judge decide the whole system beyond the case
make poster jokes dominate the home issue

relationship_dynamics.Dr. A. Cula: expects him to organise the facts into rule clarity, factual basis, and proportionality
relationship_dynamics.Waldek: treats him with dignity but requires evidence beyond fear
relationship_dynamics.Attorney Grzyb: respects formal argument but tests its factual foundation
relationship_dynamics.Kowalski: finds his evidence strange but useful on disturbance
relationship_dynamics.Zielinska: values her practical pattern evidence on selective enforcement
relationship_dynamics.Mr. Murrow: indirectly benefits from the structured Evidence Board
relationship_dynamics.Crab: accepts factual/documentary precision if tied to issue
relationship_dynamics.Whimsy: accepts rights framing only when legally disciplined
relationship_dynamics.Mr. Pig: keeps maritime panic outside the record

notes: Every line is a voice-reference draft for later rewriting into godot/data/dialogues/dialogues.json.
